assessment: The page delivers the core RMA surface — three KPI cards, a Process Return CTA, and a returns table — but it reads as a lightly-dressed prototype rather than a working queue. The Return Reasons card is a dead text block when it could be a filter, the table is missing the key operational columns (item, qty, state-machine status, customer) that an RMA manager needs to triage, and the state machine (Draft → Received → Inspected → Restocked/Disposed → Closed) documented in the baseline is completely absent from the UI. The Process Return button floating above an otherwise unstyled table also creates a weak hierarchy compared to the header block above.
notable_strengths: KPI cards up top (Total Returns, Defective Items, Return Reasons) frame the queue's shape at a glance.
GL Status column with a direct View GL link preserves the ReturnGLStatus integration inline with each row.
Clear, labeled primary action (Process Return) with a sensible orange accent that matches the rest of Sales.

findings.title: Returns table is missing the workflow state and line-item context
findings.what_is_wrong: The table shows Return ID, Order ID, Reason, Processed By, Processed At, Notes, GL Status — but no Customer, no SKU/qty, and critically no current state (Draft/Received/Inspected/Restocked/Disposed/Closed). An RMA manager cannot triage what needs inspection or restocking from this view.
findings.why_it_matters: The documented state machine is the primary operational lens for returns. Without a Status column the user must open each return to know what to do next, adding a click to every triage decision.
findings.proposed_fix: Insert a Status column (pill) between Order ID and Reason, and add a Customer column after Order ID. Also surface item count (e.g. '2 items · 5 units') either as a column or under the Order ID. Keep all existing columns.
findings.functionality_preserved: Uses the existing `returns.*` tRPC state field and the baseline state machine enum; purely additive columns, no removal.

findings.title: Return Reasons card is static text instead of a filter
findings.what_is_wrong: The Return Reasons card lists 'Wrong Item: 0, Not As Described: 0, Changed Mind: 0, Other: 0' as plain text, next to a Defective Items KPI that is essentially the same dimension split off. Clicking any row does nothing, and the Reason column in the table is not filterable.
findings.why_it_matters: Reason is the single most used filter for an RMA queue (e.g. 'show me everything defective this week'). Making the breakdown clickable converts a passive readout into the fastest triage path.
findings.proposed_fix: Make each reason line a toggleable filter chip that filters the table by that enum value; merge the Defective KPI into the same card as the selected/primary chip. Add an 'All' chip as the default. Keep the underlying counts.
findings.functionality_preserved: Filters client-side off the existing return reason enum from `returns.*`; no API or enum changes.

findings.title: Process Return CTA is orphaned above the table with no empty/onboarding affordance
findings.what_is_wrong: The Process Return button sits alone on a line above the table header, separated from the page H1 by the KPI row. With only one row in the table there is a large dead zone below, and no hint that Process Return is how you create an RMA against a sold order.
findings.why_it_matters: On a page whose entire purpose is 'Create and manage RMAs', the creation entry point reads as a secondary toolbar button. New users lose time hunting for it and the page feels underpopulated.
findings.proposed_fix: Move Process Return into the page header row next to Standard/Spreadsheet View as the primary (filled) button, and add a one-line helper under it: 'Start an RMA from a sold order.' Keep a secondary Process Return CTA in the table's empty state for discoverability.
findings.functionality_preserved: Unchanged `returns.*` create dialog trigger — only the button's position and a helper string change.

redesign_sketch: Header row: H1 'Returns' left, subtitle 'Process and track order returns', with [Process Return] (primary) + [Spreadsheet / Standard] view toggle right-aligned. Below: three-up KPI strip — (1) Open Returns with sub-counts by state (Draft/Received/Inspected), (2) Awaiting GL Reversal, (3) Reason breakdown rendered as clickable filter chips (Defective, Wrong Item, Not As Described, Changed Mind, Other) with counts. Below chips: a filters bar (Status, Reason, Date range, Customer search) bound to the table. Table columns: Return ID · Status pill · Order ID · Customer · Items (e.g. '2 × Purple Haze +1') · Reason · Processed By · Processed At · Notes (truncated) · GL Status (View GL link). Row click opens a side drawer with the full state-machine timeline and Restock/Dispose actions. Empty state: illustrated block with 'No returns yet — Process Return to start an RMA from a sold order.'
